getconf -a:
NAME
       getconf - Query system configuration variables

SYNOPSIS
       getconf [-hv] system_var
       getconf [-hv] path_var pathname

DESCRIPTION
       getconf is used to query system configuration variables.

OPTIONS
       -a     Displays all configuration variables for the current system and their values.

       -h     Help.  Display a usage message.

       -v     Indicate the specification and version for which to obtain configuration variables.

       system_var
               A system configuration variable, as defined by sysconf(3) or confstr(3).

       path_var pathname
               A system configuration variable as defined by pathconf(3). This must be used with a pathname.

CONFORMING TO
       This function is part of the base POSIX.1-2001 specification.

NOTES
       The following configuration variables are implemented in the GNU C Library.  There may be additional variables implemented in other libraries.

       _POSIX2_BC_BASE_MAX
               Maximum value for base in bc  -l  command
       _POSIX2_BC_DIM_MAX
               Maximum size of an array element of a variable-length array
       _POSIX2_BC_SCALE_MAX
               Maximum value for scale in bc  -l  command
       _POSIX2_BC_STRING_MAX
               Maximum length in characters of a string constant in bc  -l  command
       _POSIX2_CCHAR_MAX
               Maximum number of columns in a character position
       _POSIX2_CHARCLASS_NAME_MAX
               Maximum number of bytes in a character class name
       _POSIX2_LINK_MAX
               Maximum number of links to a file
       _POSIX2 ΜαX
               Maximum number of bytes in a symbolic link
       _POSIX2_NAME_MAX
               Maximum number of bytes in a filename
       _POSIX2_PATH_MAX
               Maximum number of bytes in a path name
       _POSIX2_POSIX2_VERSION
               Version number of POSIX.2
       _POSIX2_RE_DUP_MAX
               Maximum number of duplicates in a regular expression
       _POSIX2_CHAR_BIT
               Number of bits in a character
       _POSIX2_CHAR_MAX
               Maximum value for an object of type char
       _POSIX2_INT_MAX
               Maximum value for an object of type int
       _POSIX2_INT_MIN
               Minimum value for an object of type int
       _POSIX2_LONG_MAX
               Maximum value for an object of type long int
       _POSIX2_LONG_MIN
               Minimum value for an object of type long int
       _POSIX2_OFF_T_MAX
               Maximum value for an object of type off_t
       _POSIX2_SSIZE_MAX
               Maximum value for an object of type ssize_t
       _POSIX2_SSIZE_MIN
               Minimum value for an object of type ssize_t
       _POSIX2_TIME_MAX
               Maximum value for time
       _POSIX2_TIME_MIN
               Minimum value for time
       _POSIX_SYMLINK_MAX
               Maximum number of bytes in a symbolic link

       _PC_CHOWN_RESTRICTED
               Whether chown can transfer ownership of a file to a user other than the owner of the file or the superuser
       _PC_DIR_NAME_MAX
               Maximum number of bytes in a directory pathname

cat /etc/init.d/cron:
#!/bin/sh
### BEGIN INIT INFO
# Provides:          cron
# Required-Start:    $remote filesystem
# Should-Start:      $syslog
# Default-Start:     2 3 4 5
# Default-Stop:      0 1 6
# Short-Description:   Manage crontab and scheduling daemons
# Description:        Manage crontab and scheduling daemons.
### END INIT INFO

PATH=/sbin:/usr/sbin:/bin:/usr/bin
DAEMON=/usr/sbin/crond
NAME=crond
DESC="crontab and scheduling daemon"

# Include cron defaults if available
if [ -f /etc/default/cron ]; then
    . /etc/default/cron
fi

test -x $DAEMON || exit 0

case "$1" in
  start)
    # If already running, do nothing
    if test -f /var/lock/subsys/cron; then
        :
    else
        # Start cron and log it
        start-stop-daemon --start --quiet --pidfile /var/lock/subsys/cron \
          --exec $DAEMON -- -S
        echo -n "Starting cron daemon: "
        log_daemon_msg "Starting cron daemon" "crond"
        ;;
    fi
    ;;
  stop)
    # Kill cron
    if test -f /var/lock/subsys/cron; then
        log_daemon_msg "Stopping cron daemon" "crond"
        # Kill cron
        start-stop-daemon --stop --quiet --pidfile /var/lock/subsys/cron
        echo
    else
        echo -n "cron already stopped"
        echo
    fi
    ;;
  reload|force-reload)
    /etc/init.d/crontab reload
    ;;
  restart)
    ${0} stop
    ${0} start
    ;;
  status)
    status_of_proc $DAEMON cron
    exit $?
    ;;
  *)
    echo -n "Usage: `basename $0` {start|stop|force-reload|restart|status}"
    echo "/etc/init.d/$DESC $1"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

exit 0
